items.title: Burries named to All-NBA Summer League First Team
items.summary: Bucks guard and 2026 Draft No. 10 overall pick Brayden Burries was named to the NBA’s Summer League First Team. Burries, who appeared in four Summer League games, averaged 23.5 points, 4.5 rebounds, and 4.3 assists on .500/.364/.667 shooting splits. He’s the first Bucks nominee to the Summer League First Team since Sandro Mamukelashvili, now a rotational big for the Lakers, in 2022. Burries impressed throughout his time in Las Vegas. He looked like a bona fide future NBA star, getting to his spots effortlessly, converting an array of tough finishes around the rim, and creating for himself and for teammates. Burries has been the go-to three-level scorer his tape at Arizona suggested, and he also looked willing to do the dirty work: diving for loose balls and switching onto more physical defenders. He even scored a game-winner in the Bucks’ final Summer League game against Philadelphia,…

NOTE TO USER: User expressly acknowledges and agrees that, by downloading and or using this Photograph, user is consenting to the terms and conditions of the Getty Images License Agreement. Mandatory Copyright Notice: Copyright 2026 NBAE (Photo by Candice Ward/NBAE via Getty Images) | NBAE via Getty Images For the second consecutive year, the Gonzaga Bulldogs didn’t put anybody in the NBA draft. For a program that put seven guys into the NBA across the 2021 through 2024 drafts, back-to-back shutouts is an anomaly. But the draft is only the front door. Summer League is the NBA’s July proving ground: rookies, undrafted free agents, G League hopefuls, and fringe roster guys…

items.title: The Knicks can do something bigger & bolder than winning the 2026 NBA title
items.summary: NEW YORK, NY - JUNE 18: James Dolan and Jalen Brunson are seen at the New York Knicks championship ticker-tape parade and victory rally to celebrate winning the 2026 NBA Finals on June 18, 2026 in New York City. (Photo by Jose Perez/Bauer-Griffin/GC Images) 2019 is a year which will live in infamy for New York Knicks fan. That spring, despite having slogged to the league’s worst record, the Knicks were forsaken by the basketball gods, who blessed New Orleans and Memphis with lottery leaps while dropping New York to third. Kevin Durant and Kyrie Irving were long rumored to sign with them that summer. Instead of Zion, KD and Kyrie, the Knicks ended up with RJ Barrett, Julius Randle and Marcus Morris. Yet the building blocks for a title were in place: RJ, JR and IQ (via the Morris trade) landed the Knicks the second and first runners-up for 2026 Finals MVP, i.e. OG Anunoby and Karl-Anthony…

items.title: LeBron James has NBA free agency — and Lakers — in holding pattern
items.summary: It’s no secret that most of the NBA is waiting on LeBron James.Even with James’ agent, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul, saying James won’t be rushed into making a decision on which team he’ll play for next, and encouraging teams to fill out their open roster spots if they want to, much of the league’s free agency business remains on hold while James mulls his options.Until LeBron James makes up his mind on his next home, the rest of the NBA is in a freeze as so much revolves around James’ decision.As sources described to the California Post, multiple teams — and players — throughout the league are in a holding pattern until James makes his decision. It’s why James Harden remains an unrestricted free agent after declining his $42.3 million player option with the Cavaliers over three weeks ago with the expectation he’d re-sign with Cleveland on a new deal. It’s why Draymond Green…

items.title: Adam Silver pushing LeBron James into free agency decision will backfire: Draymond Green
items.summary: NBA commissioner Adam Silver may regret calling out LeBron James to decide where he will play next season.Former Warriors teammate Draymond Green said that Silver urging James to make a decision soon might only make him take longer to announce it.“Adam saying that is not going to speed up LeBron’s decision at all,” Green said on the “Draymond Green Show.” “If I know Bron well, if anything, it may slow his decision down a little more because that’s just how Bron is.NBA Commissioner Adam Silver during Fanatics Fest this past weekend. Getty ImagesGreen said that he believes James’ decision will be coming sooner rather than later.This past weekend at Fanatics Fest, Silver publicly called out James, urging him to decide where he will play next season, as it will have an impact on the NBA schedule, including opening week and Christmas Day.During a live recording of his podcast “Mind the…

items.title: Warriors rookie Yaxel Lendeborg named Summer League MVP, All Summer-League teams announced
items.summary: Yaxel Lendeborg was one of the breakout stars of Summer League. He looked like a hand-in-glove fit for the Warriors, capped off by him dropping 21 and 10 in the championship game to lead Golden State to the Summer League crown.Yaxel Lendeborg, the 11th overall pick, wins the 2026 NBA Summer League Championship Game MVP! 21 PTS 10 REB 2 STL WARRIORS W pic.twitter.com/bSa4XbBBi2— NBA (@NBA) July 20, 2026That capped off a run that saw Lendeborg named Las Vegas Summer League MVP. Over the course of six games he averaged 14.8 points, 6.8 rebounds and four assists per game.The NBA also released the All-Summer-League teams:First TeamCameron Boozer (Memphis)Brayden Burries (Milwaukee)Yaxel Lendeborg (Golden State)Meleek Thomas (Cleveland)Caleb Wilson (Chicago)Second TeamCedric Coward (Memphis)Egor Demin (Brooklyn)Khaman Maluach (Phoenix)Liam McNeeley (Charlotte)Zyon Pullin (Minnesota)It's…
